titleEn: UCAmI'19: Special Track on Ambient Intelligence for Education
excerptEn: 13th International Conference on Ubiquitous Computing and Ambient Intelligence http://mamilab.esi.uclm.es/ucami2019 Toledo, Spain December 2 to 5th, 2019 Conference Background & Goals The Ubiquitous Computing (UC) idea envisioned by Weiser in 1991, has recently evolved to a more general paradigm known as Ambient Intelligence (AmI) that represents a new generation of user-centred computing envir…
contentEn:
13th International Conference on Ubiquitous Computing and Ambient Intelligence

http://mamilab.esi.uclm.es/ucami2019

Toledo, Spain
December 2 to 5th, 2019

Conference Background & Goals
The Ubiquitous Computing (UC) idea envisioned by Weiser in 1991, has recently evolved to a more general paradigm known as Ambient Intelligence (AmI) that represents a new generation of user-centred computing environments and systems. These solutions aim to find new ways to obtain a better integration of information technology in everyday life devices and activities.

AmI environments are integrated by several autonomous computational devices of modern life ranging from consumer electronics to mobile phones. Ideally, people in an AmI environment will not notice these devices, but they will benefit from the services these solutions provide them. Such devices are aware of the people present in those environments by reacting to their gestures, actions and context. Recently the interest in AmI environments has grown considerably due to new challenges posed by society, demanding highly innovative services, such as vehicular ad hoc networks (VANET), Ambient Assisted Living (AAL), e-Health, Internet of Things and Home Automation among others. The main focus of this edition of the UCAmI Conference will be «Ambient Intelligence: Sensing, Processing and Using Environmental Information».

Publication
All accepted papers will be included in Proceedings (ISSN 2504-3900) published by MDPI. Selected papers will be published in the following journals:

  • Future Generation Computer Systems (IF(2017) = 4.639 Q1)
  • Sensors Journal (IF(2017) = 2.475 Q2)

(More journals to be announced shortly on http://mamilab.esi.uclm.es/ucami2019)

Important Dates

  • Paper submission: July 1st, 2019
  • Notifications: September 20th, 2019
  • Camera-ready version: October 20th, 2019
  • Conference dates: December 2 to 5th, 2019

TRACKS

HEALTH (AmIHEALTH) (Topics)

  • Health, wellness and disease monitoring
  • Communication, cloud, fog and network architectures for Health
  • Education and e-Learning systems in Health domains
  • Knowledge management for health: context, cognition, behaviour and user modelling
  • Data Science for Health Environments
  • Health ecosystems: frameworks, models and methodologies
  • Interaction, social and user experience within Health Environments
  • Gamification and Serious Games for Health
  • Mobile and ubiquitous Health
  • Smart technologies and algorithms for Health
  • Health Education

AMBIENT, ACTIVE AND ASSISTED LIVING (A3L) (Topics)

  • Active ageing and healthy living
  • Technologies for building age-friendly environments
  • Sustainable smart healthcare for ageing
  • Digital empowerment for ageing well
  • Wellbeing of caregivers and other healthcare professionals. Management of stress, burden and quality of life
  • Promoting autonomy and self-care at home. Integrating A3L in smart homes
  • Dealing with frailty and other disabilities to overcome daily barriers
  • Smart monitoring of chronic and non-chronic diseases
  • Promoting a healthy lifestyle at the workplace
  • Security and privacy in A3L scenarios
  • Education, training and coaching in A3L

INTERNET OF THINGS (IOT) AND SENSORS (Topics)

  • IoT enabling technologies, techniques and methods
  • IoT application and services
  • Current and future trends in IoT
  • IoT societal impacts
  • Security, privacy and trust in IoT
  • IoT interoperability, integration and performance
  • IoT experimental results and deployment scenarios
  • Human factors in IoT
  • Sensor design, integration and combination
  • Architectures, protocols and algorithms of sensor networks
  • Energy management, resource allocation, quality of service (QoS) and fault tolerance in Sensor Networks
  • Applications of hybrid sensor networks
  • Innovative real-world sensor deployments and applications
  • Sensor and actuator technologies in ambient assisted living contexts

SMART ENVIRONMENTS (Topics)

  • Adaptive Environments
  • Ambient Behavioural Analysis
  • Big data within Intelligent Environments
  • Design principle and guidelines for Intelligent Environments
  • Enabling intelligence within environments
  • Environmental Assistive Agents
  • Indirect/Inferred Sensing though environmental side channels
  • Industry 4.0: Environments, facilities and solutions
  • Intelligent Surveillance and Alerting
  • Smart Environments and e-learning process
  • Smart Classes
  • Smart Labs
  • Smart Campuses
  • LPWAN technologies/long range communications
  • Pervasive Care Solutions
  • Predictive Maintenance
  • Retroactively provisioned smart environments
  • Resilient/Self-Healing Infrastructure technologies
  • Secure Access Control Solutions
  • Smart Cities – sensing, realisation and optimisation
  • Solutions enabling intelligent design, visualisation or simulation of intelligent environments

HUMAN-COMPUTER INTERACTION (Topics)

  • Natural User Interface
  • Human-Centric Interfaces for AmI environments
  • Multimodal Interface
  • Use of context and location information in user interfaces
  • Novel input devices
  • Robot-Human interaction
  • Human-Ambient Interaction
  • Mobile Interfaces
  • Affective Interfaces
  • User modelling
  • Personalization and adaptation of user interfaces
  • Ubiquitous and ambient displays
  • User experience in Ambient Computing
  • Interaction with smart objects
  • Tangible and wearable interfaces
  • Brain-computer interaction
  • Evaluation of interfaces in Ambient and Ubiquitous Environments
  • Mobile Augmented Reality
  • New methods and methodologies for Evaluation
  • Theoretical aspects of HCI
  • Adaptive interfaces
  • Case studies for users with special needs
  • Cultural aspects of design
  • HCI & e-Learning
  • Context awareness in the learning process

AMBIENT INTELLIGENCE FOR EDUCATION (Topics)

  • AmI Environments and e-learning processes
  • AmI Campuses
  • AmI Classroom
  • AmI Labs
  • Smartphones in the classroom
  • Internet of Things in education
  • Security, Privacy and Trust in the classroom
  • Virtual, Mixed and Augmented Reality for education
  • Collaborative User Interfaces for education
  • Robotics for education
  • Wearables for education
  • Experiences and study cases

Satellite Events

  • 2nd UCAmI Cup
  • Workshop on Smart materials applications for Health
  • e-Madrid
  • Networking Session

Contact

  • grupo.mami at uclm.es
  • grupo.mami@uclm.es

titleEn: We begin the first contacts for the creation of the Network of Educational Research Institutes
excerptEn: On Monday 29 April 2019, we met at the Institute for Educational Research of the University of Barcelona – IRE (http://www.ub.edu/ire/es/): the director of that Institute, Dr Antonio Bartolomé Pina; the director of the Institute for Educational Research and Innovation of the University of the Balearic Islands – IRIE (https://irie.uib.cat/es/), Dr…
contentEn:
On Monday 29 April 2019, we met at the Institute for Educational Research of the University of Barcelona – IRE (http://www.ub.edu/ire/es/): the director of that Institute, Dr Antonio Bartolomé Pina; the director of the Institute for Educational Research and Innovation of the University of the Balearic Islands – IRIE (https://irie.uib.cat/es/), Dr Jesús Salinas Ibáñez; the director of the Educational Research Institute of the University of Girona – IRE (https://www.udg.edu/es/instituts/ire), Dr Moisès Esteban-Guitart; the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca – IUCE (https://iuce.usal.es/), Dr María José Rodríguez Conde; and the Coordinator of the PhD Programme in Education in the Knowledge Society of the University of Salamanca (https://knowledgesociety.usal.es/), Dr Francisco José García Peñalvo; with the participation, by videoconference, of the director of the Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Santiago de Compostela (http://www.usc.es/en/institutos/ice/), Dr Ana María Porto Castro. Apologies for absence were sent by Dr Carlos Marcelo García, representing the Andalusian Inter-University Institute for Educational Research, and by Dr Emilio Álvarez Arregui, director of the Institute for Educational Research and Innovation of the University of Oviedo – INIE (https://inie.uniovi.es/).

The meeting, which ran from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m., allowed us to take stock of the administrative situation of the research centres gathered there and to establish a first road map for the creation of a National Network of Educational Research Institutes, with the aim of promoting the national and international presence of these research structures in order to contribute to improving the recognition and the scientific and social impact of Spanish educational research.

As a first action, we encourage all researchers in education who carry out their research within the framework of an Educational Research Institute — or who consider it could be of interest to do so — to take part in the International Conference of Research in Education – iRED’19 (http://www.ub.edu/ired19/), which will be held in Barcelona on 4 and 5 November 2019.

titleEn: The NextMed project, a project that seeks to change the way medical professionals work
excerptEn: The competitive project NextMed: Automatic 3D modelling system for healthcare is on course after completing its first year. This project is being carried out through the joint collaboration between the Recognised Research Group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and…
contentEn:
The competitive project NextMed: Automatic 3D modelling system for healthcare is on course after completing its first year. This project is being carried out through the joint collaboration between the Recognised Research Group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and led by Professor Juan A. Juanes Méndez, in collaboration with radiologists linked to this research group, Andrés Framiñan and Juan Carlos Paniagua, together with the consortium formed by the Álava Ingenieros group and ARSOFT, the company of the Science Park of the University of Salamanca led by Santiago González Izar.

The NextMed project aims to change the way in which medical professionals work with radiological images in three dimensions.

This technological resource will use computer vision techniques, artificial intelligence and Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality technologies.

The aforementioned project has been funded by the Ministry of Economy, Industry and Competitiveness, within the state programme for research, development and innovation oriented towards the challenges of society, under the state plan for scientific and technical research and innovation.

Funded by: ERDF/Ministry of Science, Innovation and Universities – State Research Agency/. Project RTC-2017-6682-1

A project co-funded to promote technological development, innovation and quality research.

titleEn: The USAL and the CLPU organise the ERASMUS school on laser plasma diagnostics
excerptEn: The school is part of the ERASMUS+PowerLaPs project “Innovative education and training in high-power laser plasmas” which began in 2017 The University of Salamanca and the Centre for Pulsed Lasers have organised the international ERASMUS school on laser plasma diagnostics. The aim of the school is to engage students in the…
contentEn:
The school is part of the ERASMUS+PowerLaPs project “Innovative education and training in high-power laser plasmas” which began in 2017

The University of Salamanca and the Centre for Pulsed Lasers have organised the international ERASMUS school on laser plasma diagnostics. The aim of the school is to engage students in the scientific area of

Plasma Physics and high-power Lasers, training a new generation of researchers from Spain and other European countries so that they can make optimal use of laser facilities in novel applications.

This school welcomes students from 9 universities from different European Union countries, such as the United Kingdom, Greece, France and the Czech Republic, and lecturers from Europe and the United States.

The activity was organised locally by the CLPU Chair in Laser Plasma at the University of Salamanca (represented by Luca Volpe) and its opening had the backing of local bodies from the USAL and the CLPU, most notably: the director of the CLPU (Luis Roso), the Faculty of Sciences (José Miguel Mateos Roco), the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the University Institute of Fundamental Physics and Mathematics (Vicente Mateu, deputy director of the IUFFyM).

The CLPU Chair in Laser Plasma at the University of Salamanca organises this school in line with the parameters KA2 – Cooperation for innovation and the exchange of good practices and KA203 – Strategic partnerships for higher education.

Information about the school: https://www.clpu.es/es/PowerLaPs_2019

The CLPU is a unique scientific and technical infrastructure (ICTS) created in 2007 as the result of collaboration between the Ministry of Science, Innovation and Universities (at the time, the Ministry of Science and Education), the Regional Government of Castilla y León and the University of Salamanca. It is a single-site ICTS that forms part of the ICTS Map currently in force, approved on 7 October 2014 by the Council for Scientific, Technological and Innovation Policy (CPCTI). It is co-funded (its headquarters and the VEGA equipment) by the European Regional Development Fund (ERDF).

titleEn: New edition of the 2019 Teaching Training Plan
excerptEn: The Vice-Rectorate for Teaching and Quality Assessment of the University of Salamanca, through the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), is organising a new edition of the Teaching Training Plan which, in 2019, offers 1,246 places on its General Programme, 726 places on its Centre Programme and…
contentEn:
The Vice-Rectorate for Teaching and Quality Assessment of the University of Salamanca, through the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), is organising a new edition of the Teaching Training Plan which, in 2019, offers 1,246 places on its General Programme, 726 places on its Centre Programme and 15 places on the Initial Teacher Training programme (FIPU2019), spread across 80 training activities.

This edition's activities offer 1,246 places for the General Programme, 726 places for the Centre Programme and 15 places for Initial Teacher Training

The Teaching Training Plan for Teaching Staff for the 2018-2019 academic year seeks to respond to the needs identified through the evaluations of the preceding training programmes, promoted by the USAL Quality Assessment Unit, and to promote the right of public employees to continuing training and to the permanent updating of their knowledge and professional capabilities. At the same time, the training offer aims to contribute to the development of continuing training policies for the University's teaching and research staff, for the improvement and refinement of their work.

titleEn: Visit of Professors Sheila Riddel and Elisabet Weedon, of the Centre for Research in Education Inclusion and Diversity at the Moray House School of Education
excerptEn: On the afternoon of Thursday, 21 February 2018, a Seminar was delivered, in English, by the director of the Centre for Research in Education Inclusion and Diversity at the Moray House School of Education, University of Edinburgh, together with Professor Elisabet Weedon. The aim of the Seminar was to set out the importance of the complementarity…
contentEn: On the afternoon of Thursday, 21 February 2018, a Seminar was delivered, in English, by the director of the Centre for Research in Education Inclusion and Diversity at the Moray House School of Education, University of Edinburgh, together with Professor Elisabet Weedon. The aim of the Seminar was to set out the importance of the complementarity of quantitative and qualitative methods in educational research. After their talk, there was an exchange of experiences between students of the programme and thesis supervisors with the speakers. We were also visited by Raquel Casado, lecturer at the University of Burgos and a collaborator of the PhD Programme, and by Paquita González, lecturer on the PhD Programme in Education, both experts in Inclusive Education.

titleEn: The VisualMed System research group wins awards once again for its work presented at the 21st International Medical Film Festival
excerptEn: The deputy director of the IUCE and head of the recognised research group on Advanced Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), Juan A. Juanes Méndez, has received three awards at the 21st International Medical Film Festival, held in Badajoz from 19 to 24 November 2018. At the festival, more…
contentEn:
The deputy director of the IUCE and head of the recognised research group on Advanced Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), Juan A. Juanes Méndez, has received three awards at the 21st International Medical Film Festival, held in Badajoz from 19 to 24 November 2018.

More than one hundred films were presented at the festival, among which those entered by Professor Juanes received awards, with the titles: The Body in Motion (in the category of New advances and practice in medical sciences); Technological Environment for Anaesthesia Simulation (in the category of Advances in Health Informatics); and the film The Optic Pathway and the Oculomotor System.

titleEn: Visit to the IUCE by Law lecturers from the Autonomous University of Carmen, Mexico
excerptEn: Dr María Elena Reyes Monjaras, Professor of Law at the Autonomous University of Carmen, Mexico, visited us for a month at the IUCE, working on two projects entitled: “Teaching and learning law under the competence-based approach” and “Proposals to improve tutoring at the Unacar”. During the first few days, she was accompanied by…
contentEn:
Dr María Elena Reyes Monjaras, Professor of Law at the Autonomous University of Carmen, Mexico, visited us for a month at the IUCE, working on two projects entitled: “Teaching and learning law under the competence-based approach” and “Proposals to improve tutoring at the Unacar”. During the first few days, she was accompanied by Dr Ysela Rejon Jimenez, a Law lecturer at the same University.

They also attended a Seminar at the Faculty of Law and met with the Dean of Law of the USAL, Fernando Carbajo, and other lecturers, as well as with our colleague Eva María Torrecilla, a specialist in Tutoring and Educational Guidance.

titleEn: GKA EDUTECH 2019, 3rd International Conference on Technologies in Education
excerptEn: Université Paris Diderot | Paris, France | 2 and 3 May 2019 | http://tecno-educacion.com The Conference is a forum for academics focused on the crucial challenges facing education and technologies in the current landscape, bringing together local and global perspectives. It is an event that contributes…
contentEn:
Université Paris Diderot | Paris, France | 2 and 3 May 2019 | http://tecno-educacion.com

The Conference is a forum for academics focused on the crucial challenges facing education and technologies in the current landscape, bringing together local and global perspectives. It is an event that contributes to the exchange of ideas and good practice by fostering disciplinary, national, cultural, ethnic and linguistic diversity.

Registration for the conference allows speakers, in addition to attending the event, to present a paper, workshop or poster on the featured theme which in this edition will address The use of virtual environments as extended classrooms or on one of the recurring themes:

Theme 1: Teaching innovation and theoretical aspects
Theme 2: Tools for learning
Theme 3: Virtual learning environments
Theme 4: Distance and blended education models
Theme 5: Technologies for inclusive education
Theme 6: Technologies for educational assessment
Theme 7: Gamification of learning

Participants may also send their full paper to the Scientific Committee of the conference, which will review it and propose its publication in a book or journal of the Technologies in Education community.

Next deadline for submitting proposals: 19 October 2018. Proposals may be submitted in Spanish, Portuguese or English (a title and a brief abstract) via the link: http://tecno-educacion.com/congreso/envio-de-propuestas/​

Registration: once your proposal has been accepted, you can complete your registration at the following link: http://tecno-educacion.com/congreso/inscripcion/

For further information, please contact us at the following email address: soporte@tecno-educacion.com.

titleEn: Visit of Bruno César Vieira of the University of São Paulo
excerptEn: On 12 July, Bruno César Vieira, biologist and educator, visited the University Institute of Education Sciences. Bruno César Vieira is a postgraduate student at the Faculty of Education of the University of São Paulo, under the supervision of Dr María Elena Infante-Malachias. His project seeks to understand how the interpersonal relationships between teacher and students…
contentEn:
On 12 July, Bruno César Vieira, biologist and educator, visited the University Institute of Education Sciences.

Bruno César Vieira is a postgraduate student at the Faculty of Education of the University of São Paulo, under the supervision of Dr María Elena Infante-Malachias. His project seeks to understand how the interpersonal relationships between teacher and students occur in a school environment and what influence affectivity and emotions have on that relationship.

titleEn: The IUCE research group VisualMed System is awarded the RETO-COLABORACIÓN 2017 project
excerptEn: The Recognised Research Group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), affiliated with the Institute of Education Sciences and led by Professor Juan A. Juanes Méndez, has been awarded the RETO-COLABORACIÓN 2017 project, entitled NextMed “Automatic 3D modelling system for healthcare”, which will be carried…
contentEn:
The Recognised Research Group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System), affiliated with the Institute of Education Sciences  and led by Professor Juan A. Juanes Méndez, has been awarded the RETO-COLABORACIÓN 2017 project, entitled NextMed “Automatic 3D modelling system for healthcare”, which will be carried out by the consortium formed together with  Álava Ingenieros and ARSOFT, the company of the Science Park of the University of Salamanca.

The project seeks to develop a system that makes it easier for practitioners of different specialities to interpret medical images through 3D models based on radiological results. In addition, this project will develop a new Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR) system for medical use.

The aforementioned project has been funded by the Ministry of Economy, Industry and Competitiveness, within the state programme for research, development and innovation oriented towards the challenges of society, under the state plan for scientific and technical research and innovation.

titleEn: The Federal University of Mato Grosso (Brazil) visits the IUCE
excerptEn: On Tuesday 22 May 2018, the director of the international office of the Brazilian Federal University of Mato Grosso, Irene Cristina de Mello, visited the IUCE together with the director of the USAL School of Spanish for Foreigners in that city, Jorge Caballero, with the aim of establishing contacts for…
contentEn:
On Tuesday 22 May 2018, the director of the international office of the Brazilian Federal University of Mato Grosso, Irene Cristina de Mello, visited the IUCE together with the director of the USAL School of Spanish for Foreigners in that city, Jorge Caballero, with the aim of establishing contacts for the development of the USAL's Agreement with this University.

Mato Grosso has been taking part in the Universia Meeting held in Salamanca on 21 and 22 May 2018, representing its Rector.

titleEn: The University of Salamanca develops the first virtual simulator of spinal surgery for scoliosis
excerptEn: VisualMed System, a multidisciplinary research group of the University of Salamanca belonging to the IUCE and led by the professor of Human Anatomy Juan A. Juanes, and ARSOFT, a company specialising in the implementation of advanced Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality systems led by Santiago González Izard, have developed, with the collaboration of the neurosurgeons Goncalvez…
contentEn:
VisualMed System, a multidisciplinary research group of the University of Salamanca belonging to the IUCE and led by the professor of Human Anatomy Juan A. Juanes, and ARSOFT, a company specialising in the implementation of advanced Augmented Reality and Virtual Reality systems led by Santiago González Izard, have developed, with the collaboration of the neurosurgeons Goncalvez and Sánchez-Ledesma, the first virtual simulator of the surgical approach to the spine for the pathology of scoliosis.

The technological tool constitutes a “pioneering and innovative system at the global level” which offers medical students and practitioners themselves the entire “protocol of action of the surgical intervention for this back condition for the acquisition of skills and training in their medical practice”, Juan A. Juanes explains to Comunicación USAL.

One of the great advantages the system offers is that it “merges virtual and real content”, so that users can always “be guided by the images and videos recorded from a real operation, which they can even view immersively”, notes Santiago González, director of ARSOFT.

The full news item is available at http://saladeprensa.usal.es/node/112599.

titleEn: Castilla y León will lead the modernisation of Morocco's universities after the selection of the twinning project with this country submitted by the Department of Education
excerptEn: The regional Minister for Education, Fernando Rey, presented on 6 November the twinning project with the Kingdom of Morocco 'Support for the Higher Education System in Morocco as part of a rapprochement with the European Higher Education Area' ('Appui au Système de l'Enseignement supérieur au Maroc dans le cadre d'un rapprochement…
contentEn:
On 6 November, the regional Minister for Education, Fernando Rey, presented the twinning project with the Kingdom of Morocco ‘Support for the Higher Education System in Morocco as part of a rapprochement with the European Higher Education Area’ (‘Appui au Système de l’Enseignement supérieur au Maroc dans le cadre d’un rapprochement avec l’Espace européen de l’Enseignement supérieur’), launched by the European Commission in June 2017. This initiative, which has a budget of 1,200,000 euros, will run for 24 months from January 2018. During the presentation, Rey stressed that Castilla y León secured the project “thanks to the coherence and robustness of the approach set out in the proposed activities, the suitability of the profile of the experts appointed for each of the lines of work, and the experience and prestige of our region's university system”.

The full announcement can be found at http://comunicacion.jcyl.es/web/jcyl/Comunicacion/es/Plantilla100Detalle/1281372051501/_/1284761788438/Comunicacion#

titleEn: Fourth edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training
excerptEn: On Friday 26 May 2017, the fourth edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training took place, organised by the Regional Government of Castilla y León and supported by the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the Research Group in InterAction and eLearning (GRIAL)…
contentEn:
On Friday 26 May 2017, the fourth edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training took place, organised by the Regional Government of Castilla y León and supported by the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the Research Group in InterAction and eLearning (GRIAL) of the University of Salamanca. The ceremony took place in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Translation and Documentation of the University of Salamanca.

Students from different towns in Castilla y León presented 23 research projects carried out with the support of one of their teachers. The work produced will form part of proceedings under a Creative Commons licence which will shortly be published on the event website https://piiecyl.usal.es.

titleEn: Researchers from the Federico Santa María Technical University of Chile have presented the TiDYS tool at the IUCE
excerptEn: On 18 May 2017, researchers from the Federico Santa María Technical University of Chile presented at the IUCE the TiDYS tool, designed to facilitate interaction between lecturers and students in an active approach to teaching. This tool is available at https://tidys.io/
contentEn: On 18 May 2017, researchers from the Federico Santa María Technical University of Chile presented at the IUCE the TiDYS tool, designed to facilitate interaction between lecturers and students in an active approach to teaching. This tool is available at https://tidys.io/

titleEn: Call for OPEN FUTURE entrepreneurship grants
excerptEn: The USAL, together with Telefónica, the ADE and partner companies, has launched a programme to support the creation of startups called Castilla y León Open Future. They are looking for entrepreneurs who want to take on one of the technological challenges they propose, which could give rise to new companies. If you are passionate about technology…
contentEn:
The USAL, together with Telefónica, the ADE and partner companies, has launched a programme to support the creation of startups called Castilla y León Open Future.

They are looking for entrepreneurs who want to take on one of the technological challenges they propose and which could give rise to new companies.

If you are passionate about technology and have prior experience or knowledge, they will help you develop digital solutions in these fields:

1. DATA APPLIED TO MARKETING

2. IoT APPLIED TO TOYS

3. APPLICATION CONNECTIVITY (APIFICATION SERVICES)

4. SOFTWARE QUALITY AUTOMATION

In addition to the financial compensation, they offer you a complete package of services to develop your project

If you have any questions, get in touch by sending an email to cyl.openfuture@jcyl.es or by calling 983-324205.

The deadline for submitting applications ends on 31 May.

Register at www.openfuture.org/es/calls using the Fast Track access code b1taPkt7ijkLqYvb

You can download the PDF with more information here.

titleEn: Presentation of the TIDYS Platform for adaptive learning in Science and Engineering subjects
excerptEn: Tomorrow, Thursday 18 May, the presentation of the TIDYS Platform will take place in room 12 of the IUCE at 11.30 a.m., delivered by Professor Werner Creixel of the Federico Santa María Technical University. TIDYS is an open-source platform under development at the Federico Santa María Technical University in Valparaíso (Chile)…
contentEn:
Tomorrow, Thursday 18 May, the presentation of the TIDYS Platform will take place in room 12 of the IUCE at 11.30 a.m., delivered by Professor Werner Creixel of the Federico Santa María Technical University.

TIDYS is an open-source platform under development at the Federico Santa María Technical University in Valparaíso (Chile) with the collaboration of the “Física Interactiva” Educational Innovation Group of the UPM.

TIDYS facilitates real-time interaction with the audience during presentations, by means of certain tools, such as personalised navigation through the slides or putting questions to the audience, which must be answered at the moment they are posed via their devices, so that the lecturer knows in real time the percentages of correct and incorrect answers.

titleEn: The USAL Governing Council held on 26 April 2017 once again issues a favourable report on the creation of the Research Institute: IUCE
excerptEn: The research groups of the IUCE congratulate ourselves on reaching this point. contentEn:
The research groups of the IUCE congratulate ourselves on reaching this point. The Governing Council of our University, headed by the Rector Daniel Hernández, having heard the positive report of the Research Council and the Social Council, has issued a Favourable Report, once again, for the creation of this Institute as a LOU Research Institute.

From now on, it is the Department of Education of the Regional Government of Castilla y León which, through the Agency for the Quality of the University System of Castilla y León  (ACSUCYL), must issue an external evaluation report on it.

There is still some way to go. It is from this point onwards that our scientific output and research potential in Education are assessed by the external scientific community. The process is following its course. Many thanks to all the IUCE researchers for continuing to strive to contribute and for continuing to take on new challenges!
